problem_solved: Medgate provides 24/7 telemedicine access to doctors, enabling employees to receive immediate medical consultations without leaving work or home. It addresses the challenge of accessing healthcare quickly while reducing unnecessary emergency room visits.
who_buys_it: Health insurers, employers, cantonal health departments, corporate health programs
key_features: 24/7 telemedicine consultations via phone, video, and chat
Medgate Kids Line for pediatric emergencies
E-prescription and referral to specialist network of 3,800+ providers
business_model: B2B/B2B2C - Services billed through health insurance; also provides white-label telemedicine solutions to insurers and employers
why_varden_care: Strong replicability potential: telemedicine-first model can be adapted to other markets with regulatory tailwinds. The 24/7 access model with integrated specialist network is a proven playbook for employer-sponsored health benefits.

problem_solved: Kaia Health provides digital-first musculoskeletal (MSK) pain management, addressing the leading cause of workplace absenteeism and disability claims. It reduces the need for in-person physical therapy and costly procedures.
who_buys_it: Self-insured employers, health plans, TPAs, occupational health providers
key_features: AI-powered motion analysis for exercise guidance
CBT-based psychological support for chronic pain
3x ROI proven through claims studies with Fortune 150 employers
business_model: B2B SaaS - Sold to employers and health plans on per-member-per-month basis
why_varden_care: High replicability: MSK is a universal employer pain point. The digital-first approach bypasses provider capacity constraints and can scale across geographies with minimal localization.

problem_solved: EGYM Wellpass provides corporate fitness and wellness benefits, addressing employee health engagement and retention. It consolidates fragmented gym and wellness offerings into a single employer-sponsored benefit.
who_buys_it: HR departments, corporate benefits managers, occupational health teams
key_features: Access to 20,000+ fitness and wellness locations across 60+ markets
User-based pricing (only pay for active employees)
Integrated app with wellness challenges, nutrition, and prevention courses
business_model: B2B SaaS - Monthly subscription per active employee user
why_varden_care: Strong replicability: corporate fitness benefits are a proven engagement tool. The asset-light marketplace model can be replicated in any market with existing gym/studio infrastructure.

problem_solved: Arztkonsultation provides KBV-certified video consultation software, enabling doctors and occupational health providers to conduct reimbursable telemedicine consultations. It solves the technical and regulatory barriers to telemedicine adoption.
who_buys_it: Hospitals, MVZs (medical care centers), pharmacies, nursing homes, occupational health providers
key_features: KBV/GKV/KZBV certified for reimbursable video consultations
End-to-end encryption with virtual waiting room
API and white-label options for integration into existing systems
business_model: B2B SaaS - Subscription-based with API integration and white-label options
why_varden_care: High replicability in regulated markets: the playbook for achieving telemedicine certification and reimbursement eligibility is transferable to other countries with similar statutory health insurance systems.

problem_solved: Mindable Health provides digital therapeutics for anxiety disorders (panic, agoraphobia, social phobia), addressing the mental health crisis that drives workplace absenteeism and reduced productivity.
who_buys_it: Health insurers, employers, occupational health providers, mental health clinics
key_features: DiGA-certified digital therapeutics (app on prescription)
CBT-based treatment programs with 91% user recommendation rate
1 in 8 users symptom-free after 8 weeks of use
business_model: B2B/B2B2C - Reimbursed through statutory health insurance via DiGA program; also sold to employers
why_varden_care: Strong replicability: the DiGA regulatory pathway in Germany is a model for other markets developing digital therapeutics frameworks. Mental health is a universal employer concern with high ROI potential.

problem_solved: TeleClinic provides a telemedicine platform connecting patients with licensed doctors for video consultations, prescriptions, and sick leave certificates. It reduces barriers to accessing primary care for employees.
who_buys_it: Employers, health insurers, occupational health providers, direct-to-consumer
key_features: Video consultations with licensed German doctors
E-prescription and digital sick leave certificates
Integration with occupational health workflows
business_model: B2B/B2C - Per-consultation fees and corporate subscriptions
why_varden_care: Good replicability: telemedicine platforms are proven models. The focus on German regulatory compliance (e-prescriptions, sick leave) provides a playbook for other highly regulated markets.

problem_solved: DoctorBox provides a personal health management app that helps individuals organize their health data, screenings, and medications. It addresses the fragmentation of health information and preventive care tracking.
who_buys_it: Employers (as employee benefit), health insurers, direct consumers
key_features: AI-powered health assistant for personalized recommendations
Preventive screening reminders and medication tracking
Secure health document storage and pain diary
business_model: B2C/B2B2C - Freemium model with premium subscription (€2.99/month); corporate licensing
why_varden_care: Moderate replicability: personal health record apps are competitive but the preventive care focus aligns with employer wellness trends. The freemium model can be adapted to employer-sponsored benefits.

problem_solved: CompuGroup Medical provides comprehensive healthcare IT solutions including electronic health records, telemedicine, and practice management. For occupational health, it enables digitalization of workplace health services and health data management.
who_buys_it: Occupational health providers (företagshälsovård), hospitals, clinics, laboratories
key_features: Electronic health records with interoperability
Practice management and billing for occupational health
Telemedicine and remote monitoring integration
business_model: B2B SaaS - Enterprise software licensing and subscription
why_varden_care: Moderate replicability: established healthcare IT players exist in most markets, but the occupational health-specific modules are less common. Opportunity to build niche occupational health software with better UX than legacy systems.
